title: AMQP 0-9-1 Model, Virtual Hosts, Exchanges, Queues, and Bindings · Type-System Trace: Vhost Namespace Isolation
summary: A diagram sends publishers directly to shared queues, confuses exchanges with storage, ignores virtual-host isolation, and cannot explain how routing keys and bindings produce zero, one, or many destinations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the vhost namespace isolation increment in AMQP entity and routing graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use virtual hosts as separate topology and permission namespaces while recognizing they are not complete process or resource isolation.
Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Vhost Namespace Isolation · Theory Vhost Namespace Isolation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 7637 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive zero, one, or many queue destinations from exchange and binding evidence; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use virtual hosts as separate topology and permission namespaces while recognizing they are not complete process or resource isolation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Vhost Namespace Isolation contract for the supplied data.
